description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical body that bulges outward at the center and tapers toward the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held tightly together by four wide, grayish metal hoops spaced evenly along the height of the barrel. The wood has a natural, slightly weathered brown appearance, with visible grain and subtle textural variation. The top is open, revealing the hollow interior, and there are no visible handles or spouts. The overall design suggests durability and traditional craftsmanship, making it suitable for storage or decorative use in urban environments.
description: A traditional wooden barrel with metal hoops, open at the top and featuring a bulging cylindrical shape with visible wood grain.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, bulging center, and all four metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view with the same features, emphasizing the curvature and hoop placement.
description_view_2: Another angled view, similar to the others, highlighting the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Side view with consistent features, showing the open top and the full profile of the barrel.
